title: Xtra Crispy Peruvian Rice Tacu Tacu
description: A sweet and aerated Peruvian dish featuring palm sugar and crispy rice, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is FODMAP friendly and made with easily accessible ingredients.

ingredients.name: palm sugar
ingredients.notes: also known as nam tan pip

instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir in the soy sauce, salt, and pepper, and cook for about 5 minutes, until the rice is crispy and golden brown.
Stir in the chopped scallions and cook for an additional minute.
Add the palm sugar to the skillet and stir to combine, cooking for about 2 minutes, until the sugar is caramelized and the rice is crispy.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the Xtra Crispy Peruvian Rice Tacu Tacu hot, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
